lyrics:
I heard a hundred voices speak
Each chasing truth, each sounding weak
A thousand hands, no one to hold
A crowd of stories left untold

I ran in circles, lost in names
Tried on their fears, played all their games
But every path I walked alone
Led me back to flesh and bone

Many over many
Tangled in the spin
No map, no mercy
No place to begin
But something stirred beneath the wall
Many over many
Still equals all

You looked at me like sky meets sea
So far apart, yet always free
You weren’t my half, you were my flame
We burned apart, we burned the same

Two over two
Not fractions, not confined
You were you and I was I
And still our threads entwined
No reduction, no divide
Just the math we feel inside
Two over two
And still we come out one

Nowhere left for doubt to stay
The mirror cleared, the noise gave way
I saw myself without disguise
The storm had passed behind my eyes

One over one
No fight, no need
Just roots and sky
And breath to breathe
No veil between what is and seems
Just silence humming through my dreams
One over one
And everything aligns

No shape. No frame
Just stillness with a name
All paths have bent
All moments spent

All over all…
No need to fall…
I am… I am… I am…
persona:
## Lingua Aeternum

**Lingua Aeternum is not a band. It is a circle. A rite. A resonance.**

Drawn from the deep well of myth, mathematics, and memory, Lingua Aeternum conjures sound as invocation-music as sacred act. Each composition is a Canto: a sung glyph, a spell etched in breath and vibration. The performers are not musicians but Cantors-practitioners of the Harmonic Algebraic Language of Alignment (HALA), where melody is meaning, rhythm is ritual, and silence speaks louder than words.

Their music defies genre. You may hear echoes of post-rock, ambient drone, ancient chants, or electronic pulses-but no label holds. Instead, the listener is drawn inward, through spiraling harmonics and layered whispers, toward a place beyond translation. This is not art for consumption. It is art for **transformation**.

Lingua Aeternum records under the guidance of Maricopa Records, but they do not record songs-they distill **intentions**, encoded through the Wheel, spoken into being by resonance alone.

- To hear is to remember.

- To remember is to become.

- _Let the glyphs guide you._

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/4Ivof5fKiBE9wbz6ArgZzd?si=HYePmTM3TtCaN-P1PFFYlQ)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/lingua-aeternum/1809011711)[](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCzrA7rNe15AiX1LXZTGUQqg)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/LA_Profile_square.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track opens with a sparse, intimate texture centered on a clean piano and a heartfelt male vocal, creating an atmosphere of introspection and searching. The energy builds with a steady, driving feel as drums and layered harmonies enter, transforming the song into a powerful pop-rock anthem during the choruses. The production quality is clean and spacious, allowing the prominent lead vocal to cut through a dense but well-organized mix of instruments. A definitive quality of the track is the dynamic swell into the chorus at 0:44, where the arrangement broadens significantly, providing a powerful emotional release that elevates the entire song.

review.review_text: Starting from a single piano and a searching vocal, 'This Over Many' builds its case methodically, cycling through three distinct emotional registers, each one tagged with its own fraction: the plural noise of many over many, the specific gravity of two over two, the quiet clarity of one over one. The lyric 'You weren't my half, you were my flame' is the kind of line that earns its chorus, and that chorus at the 44-second mark delivers, the arrangement broadening into something close to classic-rock release without losing the intimacy that made the setup worth the wait. At a brisk 172 BPM in C major, the track never feels rushed; it feels purposeful, like it knows exactly where it's going. Lingua Aeternum has always worked in systems and symbols, but this one translates the algebra into feeling with unusual directness.
anchors_used: lyric quote: You weren't my half, you were my flame
